title: windows-supervisor.ts child-process protocol logic never executed by any test
evidence: windows-supervisor.ts is the inert Windows launch supervisor that parses a bounded launch record (parseLaunch), spawns the real Pi child, wires stdio/fd3 capability pipes, and fail-closes (exitCode 70) on any parse/spawn/pipe error. No test imports or executes this module. The only reference in the suite is `test/process-tree.test.ts:185-188`, which does `readFileSync(new URL("../src/windows-supervisor.ts", ...))` and asserts the source text `not.toContain('child.kill("SIGKILL")')` — a string-presence check on source code, not an execution of the module. `parseLaunch`'s bounds (256 args, 65_536 byte arg cap, exactObject key check), `boundedRead`'s overflow rejection, `finalizeExit`'s pipe-teardown ordering, and the `failClosed`/`REFUSED` path are all unexercised by any test that actually runs the code.
impact: This module is the last line of defense before an arbitrary child process is spawned on Windows with the real Pi CLI's stdio wired through unauthenticated named pipes; a regression in parseLaunch's validation, the exactObject key check, or the fail-closed error path would ship undetected since CI's python jobs are ubuntu-only and no vitest test executes this file at all.
recommendation: Add a unit test that imports/executes windows-supervisor.ts (e.g. via child_process with piped fds, or refactor parseLaunch/boundedRead/exactObject into an importable, directly-testable module) covering: malformed launch JSON, oversized launch payload, wrong key set, control byte overflow, double START, and the REFUSED/exit-70 fail-closed path.
acceptance_criteria: A test exists that executes (not just greps) windows-supervisor.ts logic
parseLaunch rejects invalid/oversized/relative-path launch records under test
the failClosed/REFUSED path is exercised and asserted
